title: Kernels
description: Quick check on kernels, dual mode operation, and kernel structures.

questions.prompt: What is the name of the OS component that sits between hardware and user processes, runs at all times, and is loaded into memory at startup?
What is the name of the bit that indicates whether the CPU is currently in user or kernel mode?
Which of the following are listed as advantages of a monolithic kernel (as opposed to a microkernel)?
options: Easier to extend with new functionality

questions.prompt: Which of the following are disadvantages of a layered kernel structure?
options: Performance: syscalls may traverse multiple layers

questions.prompt: Match each OS structure to its defining characteristic.
right: Only essential components in the kernel; non-essentials run as user-level programs
Core services at startup; extra functionality loaded dynamically while running
Layer K uses services from K-1 and provides services to K+1
All OS functions built into the kernel itself

questions.prompt: Select all TRUE statements about microkernels.
options: The kernel contains only process management, memory management, and inter-process communication
They typically have higher syscall overhead than monolithic kernels
They are harder to extend than monolithic kernels
They tend to be more secure and reliable than monolithic kernels
All OS services run in kernel mode

questions.prompt: What is the name of the region of memory in which the kernel executes, protected from user processes and accessed via system calls?
